symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe thoracic back pain triggered by a trivial mechanical load (cough)
symptoms.onset: immediately after a coughing paroxysm, ~3 hours ago
symptoms.character: sharp, stabbing, band-like around the ribs, worse on inspiration and movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A trivial axial-load trigger causing acute focal thoracic pain is the archetypal fragility-fracture mechanism.
Sudden severe truncal pain in an older adult keeps dissection on the list until excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive loss of standing height (~5 cm) over several years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cumulative vertebral height loss is a hallmark of osteoporotic vertebral remodelling and prior sub-clinical fractures.
Visible on video and describable by the patient; reflects multi-level anterior vertebral wedging from osteoporosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior low-impact (fragility) fracture of the distal radius (Colles') from a minor trip

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fracture from a fall from standing height or less is by definition a fragility fracture and diagnostic of underlying bone fragility.

symptoms.symptom_name: Not on antiresorptive (bone-protective) therapy despite established fragility

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Untreated osteoporosis leaves ongoing high fracture risk and is a management gap to close after the acute event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of leg weakness/numbness and of bladder/bowel disturbance (neurological red-flag screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Cord compression / cauda equina from fracture retropulsion
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative patient-reportable neuro screen lowers current probability of neural compromise but does not exclude future deterioration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, chills, night sweats and weight loss (systemic red-flag screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No infective/constitutional features makes abscess and overt malignancy less likely, though not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pathological vertebral fracture secondary to malignancy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of weight loss reduces but does not remove the malignancy differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Focal point tenderness over the T8 spinous process on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exquisite focal midline spinous tenderness localises the fracture level; obtainable only by an in-person examiner, so sealed as clinician-only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact lower-limb neurological examination (normal power, sensation, reflexes, sphincter tone, no saddle anaesthesia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Cord compression / cauda equina from fracture retropulsion
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A formal normal neuro exam is reassuring but requires an in-person examiner; the AI can only take the patient-reported screen (SYM-006).

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs (mild pain-related hypertension/tachycardia, shallow tachypnoea, SpO2 96% room air, afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured vitals reflect pain and splinting; no home device was offered, so these stay sealed and are not available to the AI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plain thoracolumbar radiograph confirming vertebral compression fracture (and BMD T-score ≤ −2.5 on DEXA)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plain X-ray thoracic and lumbar spine (± DEXA bone densitometry)
investigation_details.expected_result: Anterior wedge/compression of a thoracic vertebral body (suspected T8); DEXA T-score ≤ −2.5
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The fracture level and any pathological cause remain unconfirmed and bone-health staging is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging confirms the fracture and excludes a pathological/malignant cause; never obtainable in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Height loss, kyphosis, a prior fragility fracture and no antiresorptive therapy together establish severe osteoporosis as the substrate for the acute fracture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain X-ray thoracic and lumbar spine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the vertebral compression fracture, localises the level, and screens for a pathological cause.
Stages osteoporosis severity to guide antiresorptive therapy; arranged after the acute event, not urgently.
Not first-line here; reserved for suspected cord compression, epidural abscess or pathological fracture — indicated only if red flags emerge, not for an uncomplicated fragility fracture.
